title: Entryway cabinet
description: Solid black walnut. The grain runs continuous across the entire front — the top drawer and the doors below it — uninterrupted by pulls or knobs: a single recessed finger-pull worked into the center opens them all. On tapered legs.

images.alt: Black walnut entryway cabinet with grain running continuous across the drawers and doors, a potted plant on top

title: Sideboard
description: A mid-century sideboard with bold striped grain — one hinged cabinet door and two sliding doors, a raised gallery rail, and a lower display shelf.

images.alt: Mid-century black walnut sideboard with a cabinet door and two sliding doors, a gallery rail, and a lower shelf
SketchUp model of the sideboard
images.caption: The SketchUp model it was built from.

title: Bench from old chairs
description: Built for a neighbor, to her own design, from two of her old chairs — their backs joined with shaped arms into a single two-seat bench, then painted white.

images.alt: White-painted two-seat bench built from a pair of antique chair backs
Paper-and-tape mockup planning the bench from two chairs
images.caption: Her design, mocked up from the two chairs.

title: Scrapwood chair
description: More an experiment in bending plywood than polished furniture — scrap ply coaxed into a contoured back and seat on splayed dowel legs.

images.alt: Plywood dining chair with a contoured back and seat on splayed dowel legs

title: Under-stair storage
description: A pull-out drawer of angled shoe racks, faced in black walnut with a recessed finger pull, tucked into the dead space under the stairs.

images.alt: Pull-out shoe-rack drawer faced in black walnut, fitted into the space under the stairs

title: TV stand
description: A low credenza — four drawers with cabinet pulls flanking an open center cubby, on angled mid-century legs.

images.alt: Low black walnut TV credenza with four drawers and an open center cubby on angled legs
Colored SketchUp render of the TV stand
images.caption: The SketchUp design.

title: Wall lantern
description: A Japanese-style lantern: a padauk frame with a maple lattice and shoji-paper panels, under a peaked, mitered roof — reverse-engineered from a photo of a commercial one and built from scratch over a few weekends.

images.alt: Completed padauk lantern with maple lattice and shoji panels on a workbench
Reference photo of the commercial lantern the design was based on
images.caption: The commercial lantern I reverse-engineered the design from.

images.alt: SketchUp model of the wall lantern
images.caption: The SketchUp model I drew from that photo.

images.alt: The lantern installed and lit beside a front door at night
images.caption: Installed and lit by the front door.

images.alt: Milled padauk slats with cut notches on a workbench
images.caption: Milled padauk slats, notched for the frame.

images.alt: The padauk frame skeleton during glue-up
images.caption: The frame during glue-up.

images.alt: Four assembled maple lattice panels
images.caption: The four maple lattice panels.

images.alt: Four pierced padauk pieces for the roof ridge
images.caption: Pierced padauk pieces for the roof ridge.

images.alt: Shaped wall-mount bracket and base parts
images.caption: The shaped wall bracket and base parts.

title: Mailbox
description: A wall mailbox — a padauk frame and letter slot over a maple-plywood drop-down door, its palette chosen to match the wall lantern by the door.

images.alt: Wall-mounted mailbox with a padauk frame over a maple-plywood drop-down door
images.caption: Mounted by the front door.

images.alt: A hand pulling open the mailbox's drop-front compartment
images.caption: The drop-front compartment, open.

images.alt: The mailbox and front panel on a workbench before mounting
images.caption: Before mounting.

title: LEGO shadow box
description: A black walnut shadow box with a 3×4 grid of bays, built to display a set of LEGO BrickHeadz.

images.alt: Black walnut grid shadow box displaying LEGO BrickHeadz figures, beside flowers
Two filled black walnut shadow boxes on a mantel
images.caption: A pair of them, filled, on the mantel.

images.alt: Close-up of the shadow box's dovetailed corner joinery
images.caption: Dovetailed corner detail.

title: Mosaic coasters
description: Hardwood offcuts glued up into mosaic blocks, then sliced thin into coasters — a whole batch of sets, each paired with a color-matched 3D-printed stand.

images.alt: Several sets of mosaic end-grain wood coasters on color-matched printed stands
A mosaic coaster set on a black printed stand
A mosaic coaster set on a terracotta printed stand
A mosaic coaster set on a green printed stand
A coaster set with a dark end-grain mosaic on a white stand
A coaster set with a red and purple mosaic on a white stand
The full batch of coaster sets on color-matched stands, with a stack of spares
images.caption: The full batch, with a stack of spares.

title: MagSafe stand
description: A maple face on a padauk wedge base, with a recessed cable channel and magnets set flush to hold the MagSafe puck. Two brass rods through the base double as the joint between the two halves and as ballast to keep it steady.

images.alt: Maple and padauk MagSafe stand, empty
images.caption: The maple face and padauk base.

images.alt: The MagSafe stand holding a charger puck
images.caption: Holding the MagSafe puck.

images.alt: 3D CAD render of the MagSafe stand
images.caption: The CAD model.

images.alt: Dimensioned CAD drawing of the charger cutout
images.caption: Dimensioned drawing of the charger cutout.

title: Tissue boxes
description: An experiment in different materials and joinery — tissue-box covers in black walnut, shedua, bubinga, and a herringbone-patterned plywood, each trying a different wood and a different joint.

images.alt: Black walnut cube tissue-box cover on a workbench
images.caption: Black walnut.

images.alt: Shedua cube tissue-box cover on a workbench
images.caption: Shedua.

images.alt: Herringbone-patterned plywood cube tissue-box cover
images.caption: Herringbone-patterned plywood.

images.alt: Bubinga and maple rectangular tissue box with box-jointed corners
images.caption: Bubinga and maple, box-jointed.
